pull.title: dbwrapper/walletdb: reuse scratch `DataStream` buffers
pull.body:
### Problem
`CDBIterator::GetValue()` cannot use `SpanReader` the same way as `::GetKey()` does because values are deobfuscated in place before deserialization, so it still needs an owning mutable buffer.
However, the current path allocates a fresh `DataStream` for every value read.

The same pattern exists in a few stateful DB helpers: the owning object is already reused across repeated operations, but the serialization scratch space is rebuilt or manually cleared on each call.

### Fix
Add `ScopedDataStreamUsage`, a small scope helper for caller-owned scratch streams.
It asserts that the stream is empty on entry (making accidental re-entry or concurrent use of the same scratch stream fail fast), and clears it on scope exit.

This keeps the const read-side `CDBWrapper` helpers unchanged, since those methods can be called concurrently on the same wrapper and should keep using local streams.

The production changes are preceded by tests covering repeated reuse on the same owning objects, including a failed iterator value decode followed by a successful read from the same iterator entry.

### Context
Follow-up to #35128, #34483 and #35025.

### Reproducer
`gettxoutsetinfo` gets an additional ~6% speedup on top of the previous iterator-key optimization:

<details><summary>2026-04-24 | gettxoutsetinfo | i9-ssd | x86_64 | Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz | 16 cores | 62Gi RAM | xfs | SSD</summary>

```bash
COMMITS="2d5ab09f0dca4bfec0b365f5f431def2c0c9d70f 9e5fd595ae8ebeec74678c31a547fbc14f87bf89"; \
BASE_DIR="/mnt/my_storage"; DATA_DIR="$BASE_DIR/BitcoinData"; LOG_DIR="$BASE_DIR/logs"; \
mkdir -p "$LOG_DIR" && \
(echo ""; for c in $COMMITS; do git cat-file -e "$c^{commit}" 2>/dev/null || git fetch -q origin "$c" || exit 1; git log -1 --pretty='%h %s' "$c" || exit 1; done) && \
(echo "" && echo "$(date -I) | gettxoutsetinfo | $(hostname) | $(uname -m) | $(lscpu | grep 'Model name' | head -1 | cut -d: -f2 | xargs) | $(nproc) cores | $(free -h | awk '/^Mem:/{print $2}') RAM | $(df -T $BASE_DIR | awk 'NR==2{print $2}') | $(lsblk -no ROTA $(df --output=source $BASE_DIR | tail -1) | grep -q 1 && echo HDD || echo SSD)"; echo "") && \
hyperfine \
  --sort command \
  --runs 3 \
  --export-json "$BASE_DIR/gettxoutsetinfo-$(sed -E 's/([a-f0-9]{8})[a-f0-9]* ?/\1-/g;s/-$//'<<<"$COMMITS")-$(date +%s).json" \
  --parameter-list COMMIT ${COMMITS// /,} \
  --prepare "killall -9 bitcoind 2>/dev/null || true; rm -f $DATA_DIR/debug.log; git clean -fxd && git reset --hard {COMMIT} && \
    cmake -B build -G Ninja -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release && ninja -C build bitcoind bitcoin-cli -j$(nproc) && \
    ./build/bin/bitcoind -datadir=$DATA_DIR -connect=0 -listen=0 -dnsseed=0 -coinstatsindex=0 -txindex=0 -blockfilterindex=0 -daemon -printtoconsole=0; \
    ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR -rpcwait getblockcount >/dev/null" \
  --conclude "./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR stop 2>/dev/null || true; killall bitcoind 2>/dev/null || true; sleep 10; \
    grep -q 'Done loading' $DATA_DIR/debug.log && grep 'Bitcoin Core version' $DATA_DIR/debug.log | grep -q \"\$(git rev-parse --short=12 {COMMIT})\"; \
    cp $DATA_DIR/debug.log $LOG_DIR/gettxoutsetinfo-{COMMIT}-$(date +%s).log" \
  "./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=$DATA_DIR -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null"

2d5ab09f0d Merge bitcoin/bitcoin#35124: bench: fix benchmark fixtures and setup checks
cb63e158d9 walletdb: reuse batch scratch streams

2026-04-24 | gettxoutsetinfo | i9-ssd | x86_64 | Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz | 16 cores | 62Gi RAM | xfs | SSD

Benchmark 1: ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 2d5ab09f0dca4bfec0b365f5f431def2c0c9d70f)
  Time (mean ± σ):     60.063 s ±  1.623 s    [User: 0.001 s, System: 0.002 s]
  Range (min … max):   59.020 s … 61.933 s    3 runs

Benchmark 2: ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = cb63e158d9ae8276dbe54bba0b0cf8f35378ec71)
  Time (mean ± σ):     56.853 s ±  0.179 s    [User: 0.002 s, System: 0.001 s]
  Range (min … max):   56.675 s … 57.033 s    3 runs

Relative speed comparison
        1.06 ±  0.03  ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = 2d5ab09f0dca4bfec0b365f5f431def2c0c9d70f)
        1.00          ./build/bin/bitcoin-cli -datadir=/mnt/my_storage/BitcoinData -rpcclienttimeout=0 -named gettxoutsetinfo hash_type='none' use_index='false' >/dev/null (COMMIT = cb63e158d9ae8276dbe54bba0b0cf8f35378ec71)
```

</details>

tree 27d164d492d00dd8679dd23d03b115548952fb34
parent 2d5ab09f0dca4bfec0b365f5f431def2c0c9d70f
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061598 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065248 +0200

streams: add `ScopedDataStreamUsage`

Some DB paths reuse `DataStream` scratch buffers across calls or loop iterations.

Add a small scope helper that asserts a scratch stream is empty on entry and clears it on scope exit.

This is safe because the helper only operates on caller-owned scratch streams and restores the same empty-stream state that local temporary streams provided before reuse.

tree fcef7bde26bb23300857d222014484193fa82754
parent 432a4c8f3c172f5a9d887ddcfdc3d7b0aa175c47
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061608 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065249 +0200

test: cover repeated dbwrapper stream use

The upcoming dbwrapper changes reuse scratch `DataStream` buffers on `CDBBatch` and `CDBIterator` instead of creating fresh temporary streams for every call.

Extend dbwrapper tests to reuse a `CDBBatch` after `Clear()`, seek multiple times on the same iterator, and attempt a too-large `CDBIterator::GetValue()` decode before reading the same value successfully.

This locks down the safety contracts needed by the following production commits:
* `CDBBatch` scratch streams must be clean before reuse.
* Iterator seek scratch must be reusable.
* Failed iterator value deserialization must only consume scratch stream state while leaving the current iterator entry readable.

verification.payload:
tree 4095968db61aa8dedd620f7079286c2367d3868f
parent 288e5712ea2418fb3e4c09304dfdb4626ebf93e2
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061657 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065249 +0200

dbwrapper: guard `CDBBatch` scratch streams

`CDBBatch` already keeps key and value serialization buffers as reusable members, but each call manually reserved and cleared them.

Wrap `Write()` and `Erase()` scratch-buffer use in `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so the streams must start empty and are cleared on every exit path.

This removes the now-unused `DBWRAPPER_PREALLOC_VALUE_SIZE` constant; `DBWRAPPER_PREALLOC_KEY_SIZE` stays because the const read-side helpers still use local key streams.

This is safe because `WriteImpl()` and `EraseImpl()` use the serialized bytes only for the immediate `leveldb::WriteBatch::Put()` and `leveldb::WriteBatch::Delete()` calls; the batch stores its own copy and does not retain pointers into the scratch streams after those calls return.

`Clear()` can assert that the scratch streams are clean because it only resets the underlying LevelDB write batch and callers invoke it after the guarded helpers have returned.

Wrap `Write()` and `Erase()` scratch-buffer use in `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so the streams must start empty and are cleared on every exit path.

This removes the now-unused `DBWRAPPER_PREALLOC_VALUE_SIZE` constant; `DBWRAPPER_PREALLOC_KEY_SIZE` stays because the const read-side helpers still use local key streams.

This is safe because `WriteImpl()` and `EraseImpl()` use the serialized bytes only for the immediate `leveldb::WriteBatch::Put()` and `leveldb::WriteBatch::Delete()` calls; the batch stores its own copy and does not retain pointers into the scratch streams after those calls return.

`Clear()` can assert that the scratch streams are clean because it only resets the underlying LevelDB write batch and callers invoke it after the guarded helpers have returned.

tree 27f91093a31480e5d1ef167037e7f569c659c29d
parent 43146157b58893ab5c1b60bccf4035b9608991dd
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061645 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065250 +0200

dbwrapper: reuse iterator scratch streams

`CDBIterator::Seek()` and `CDBIterator::GetValue()` both serialize or copy bytes only to use them immediately on the current iterator object.

Keep those scratch buffers on `CDBIterator` and guard each use with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` instead of allocating fresh `DataStream` objects for every seek key or value read.

This is safe because `CDBIterator` is non-copyable and accessed through a single owning iterator object, `CDBIterator::SeekImpl()` only needs the serialized key for the immediate LevelDB seek call, and `CDBIterator::GetValue()` still copies the LevelDB value bytes into an owning stream before in-place deobfuscation.

The guard resets both scratch streams on success and failure, and the preceding test covers repeated seeks plus failed value deserialization followed by a successful read from the same iterator entry.

Co-authored-by: Andrew Toth <andrewstoth@gmail.com>

tree 40783b1119b7c7ad13c554409aa169c894773cee
parent 4e9fd50b38265ed53b65b2d915b88b651b6d92ff
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061671 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065250 +0200

test: cover repeated wallet batch stream use

The upcoming `DatabaseBatch` change reuses the same key and value `DataStream` buffers across multiple operations on one batch object.

Extend the existing write-error test to keep using the same batch through mixed success and failure paths across repeated `Exists()`, `Write()`, `Erase()`, and `Read()` calls.

This locks down the behavior that matters for scratch-stream reuse: mixed outcomes must leave the batch usable for later operations on the same handle.

verification.payload:
tree 45b91498f0cad6030ef09135114cbdb6da1a9c61
parent 9e6a9708a4449a23079b10bad7d0bf853fc1da03
author Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777061683 +0200
committer Lőrinc <pap.lorinc@gmail.com> 1777065250 +0200

walletdb: reuse batch scratch streams

`DatabaseBatch::Read()`, `DatabaseBatch::Write()`, `DatabaseBatch::Erase()`, and `DatabaseBatch::Exists()` serialized keys and values into fresh local `DataStream` objects on every call.

Move those staging buffers onto `DatabaseBatch` and guard them with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so repeated operations on the same stateful batch handle can reuse the existing allocations.

This is safe because `DatabaseBatch` is non-copyable mutable state, these methods are non-const, and the backend virtual calls consume the serialized bytes before returning instead of retaining pointers into the caller-owned streams.

The guard clears the moved-from streams on every exit path, so later batch operations still start from the same empty-stream contract that the old local temporaries provided.

events.message:

`DatabaseBatch::Read()`, `DatabaseBatch::Write()`, `DatabaseBatch::Erase()`, and `DatabaseBatch::Exists()` serialized keys and values into fresh local `DataStream` objects on every call.

Move those staging buffers onto `DatabaseBatch` and guard them with `ScopedDataStreamUsage` so repeated operations on the same stateful batch handle can reuse the existing allocations.

This is safe because `DatabaseBatch` is non-copyable mutable state, these methods are non-const, and the backend virtual calls consume the serialized bytes before returning instead of retaining pointers into the caller-owned streams.

The guard clears the moved-from streams on every exit path, so later batch operations still start from the same empty-stream contract that the old local temporaries provided.

A summary of reviews will appear here.
<!--5faf32d7da4f0f540f40219e4f7537a3-->

comments.body: What is the purpose of having a separate member for value and key here? `Seek` and `GetValue` cannot be called concurrently, and the `ScopedDataStreamUsage` will ensure a single `DataStream` will always be cleared before either method is called.

I'm not sure the wallet db code should be touched here. These methods are all moving `m_key` and `m_value`, which can steal the underlying heap buffer. If they do, then that negates the purpose of keeping the `DataStream` members and not reserving here.

(And if they don't, then that's an implementation detail of `SQLiteBatch` which we can't depend on)
